key_insight: In bilayer InSe, local stacking energy is not set just by the interfacial Se motif; many-body electronic effects make full registry crucial and create much larger energy corrugation than DFT predicts.
engineering_relevance: Moiré and twisted-layer models that rely on DFT local-registry energetics may underestimate relaxation, domain formation, and electronic reconstruction, leading to inaccurate structure-property predictions.
method: Benchmarked DFT local-registry energetics against diffusion quantum Monte Carlo (DMC).
Compared AB, AAr, and ABr stackings that share the same interfacial Se registry.
Assessed energy separations and charge-density responses across stackings.
evidence_signal: DMC separated the nominally near-degenerate DFT stackings by 8(5) and 41(4) meV/f.u., with a 60(7) meV/f.u. spread between the most and least stable registries.
watch_item: The benchmark is specific to bilayer InSe, so applying DFT local-registry parameters to other materials may still require system-specific quantum benchmarking.
paper_title: Many-body benchmarking of DFT local-registry energetics in bilayer InSe
